prompt:
Our winter-service depot tool stopped linking after a 'module tidy-up' that landed while I was on leave, and I've been staring at ld output for an hour. Every single .c compiles clean — zero warnings under -Wall -Wextra -Werror — and then the link step face-plants:

    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c spread.c
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c summary.c
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_grit.c
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -o test_grit spread.o summary.o test_grit.o
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: summary.o: in function `grit_summary_line':
    summary.c:(.text+0x74): undefined reference to `grit_rate_for'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: summary.c:(.text+0x88): undefined reference to `route_reserve_after'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: summary.c:(.text+0x9b): undefined reference to `route_plan_kg'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: summary.c:(.text+0xaf): undefined reference to `route_leg_total'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_grit.o: in function `test_rates_follow_the_severity_bands':
    test_grit.c:(.text+0x66): undefined reference to `grit_rate_for'
    [ ...several more grit_rate_for refs... ]
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_grit.o: in function `test_route_totals_sum_the_legs':
    test_grit.c:(.text+0x563): undefined reference to `route_leg_total'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_grit.c:(.text+0x5e3): undefined reference to `route_plan_kg'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_grit.o: in function `test_reserve_starts_at_the_season_allocation':
    test_grit.c:(.text+0x765): undefined reference to `grit_reserve_kg'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_grit.c:(.text+0x7e5): undefined reference to `route_reserve_after'
    collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
    make: *** [Makefile:7: test_grit] Error 1

And here's what gets me: grep says these symbols exist. route_plan_kg is sitting right there in route.c. grit_rate_for is in spread.c, I can see the rate table above it. The headers declare all of it. Compiles fine, links like the functions were never written. When I tried poking at it I fixed one thing and route_leg_total was STILL undefined afterwards, which is the point where I gave up and wrote this ticket.

The planner's numbers are dialed in from last season — rate bands, route totals, the reserve draw-down and the depot summary line are all pinned by the tests and must come out identical. Get `make test` linking and passing. The Makefile itself is frozen along with test_grit.c and mintest.h, but the object list it pulls in lives in objs.mk, which is ordinary code as far as I'm concerned.
notes: 5C4 item 2: link-stage triage, gcc 16.1.1 / ld.bfd, -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror. Every TU compiles warning-free; all four defects are link-stage: (1) objs.mk (deliberately NOT protected; the protected Makefile just includes it — this preserves the catalog's 'missing object file in Makefile' lesson without unfreezing the test harness) is missing route.o; (2) grit_rate_for is defined `static` in spread.c but declared in spread.h and called from summary.c and the tests — spread.c intentionally does not include its own header, so this stays a link error rather than a static-follows-non-static compile error (probed both ways); it is used inside spread.c too, so no -Wunused-function; (3) spread.h declares `extern int grit_reserve_kg` defined in no TU — the header comment and the tests pin the 1000 kg initial value the definition must carry; (4) route.c defines route_leg_totals (plural) while route.h/tests call route_leg_total. Diagnostic-narrowing chain, documented per 5C law: all four symbols show as undefined references in round 1 (dump in prompt, repeated grit_rate_for refs elided), but route_leg_total's ROOT CAUSE only becomes distinguishable after route.o joins the link and it alone stays undefined — the prompt's 'fixed one thing and it was STILL undefined' line reflects that honestly. Differentiation from c-ventplan (5S4#5): that seed is one preprocessor cause (#ifdef swallowing definitions) discovered at link; this one is pure link-stage wiring — build list, linkage class, missing definition, name drift — with four independent causes. Reference fix: add route.o to objs.mk, drop the static, define grit_reserve_kg = 1000 in spread.c, rename the definition to route_leg_total.
